extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters filed by and against each entity. For criminal proceedings, all Against rows are Nil, while the Company has one By matter; therefore I counted zero criminal proceedings against. For statutory/regulatory proceedings, all Against and By rows are Nil, so I counted zero regulatory actions against. For tax proceedings, the Company has one Against matter and the subsidiary has Nil, while the Company has one By tax matter; therefore I counted one tax proceeding against and excluded the By matter. For the aggregate amount against the Company and subsidiary, I summed the quantified Against amounts of ₹27.44 million for the Company and ₹20.00 million for the subsidiary, excluding the Company’s ₹1.07 million By amount, resulting in ₹47.44 million; the table’s ₹28.51 million Total includes By matters and therefore was not used for the directional aggregate. The contingent-liabilities figure is not disclosed in this risk section. Customer concentration is company-wide for Fiscal 2020 and is measured as a percentage of revenue from operations: top five customers 43.95% and top ten customers 53.72%. Litigation amounts are sourced in ₹ million and converted to crore by dividing by 10: ₹47.44 million ÷ 10 = ₹4.744 crore.
